content.problem_latex:
The value of the side of a square is a prime number and which of the following statements is true?
(A) The value of its area is a prime number
(B) The value of its area is a composite number
(C) It can not be determined whether the value of its area is a prime number or not.
content.problem_plain: The value of the side of a square is a prime number and which of the following statements is true? (A) The value of its area is a prime number (B) The value of its area is a composite number (C) It can not be determined whether the value of its area is a prime number or not.

answer.final_raw: The value of its area is a composite number
answer.final_normalized: The value of its area is a composite number
candidates: The value of its area is a composite number

content.problem_latex:
Which of these statements is always true?
(A) If there are at least two twos in a number then it must be a multiple of two.
(B) If a number ends in five then it must be a multiple of $25$.
(C) If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four.
(D) If a number is an even number then it must be a multiple of $4$.
(E) If the sum of all the digits in a number is a multiple of five then the number must be a multiple of five.
content.problem_plain: Which of these statements is always true? (A) If there are at least two twos in a number then it must be a multiple of two. (B) If a number ends in five then it must be a multiple of 25 . (C) If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four. (D) If a number is an even number then it must be a multiple of 4 . (E) If the sum of all the digits in a number is a multiple of five then the number must be a multiple of five.

answer.final_raw: If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four.
answer.final_normalized: If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four.
candidates: If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four.

search.problem_text: Which of these statements is always true? (A) If there are at least two twos in a number then it must be a multiple of two. (B) If a number ends in five then it must be a multiple of 25 . (C) If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four. (D) If a number is an even number then it must be a multiple of 4 . (E) If the sum of all the digits in a number is a multiple of five then the number must be a multiple of five.

choices.label: A
choices.content: If there are at least two twos in a number then it must be a multiple of two.

choices.label: B
choices.content: If a number ends in five then it must be a multiple of $25$.

choices.label: C
choices.content: If the last two digits in a number are a multiple of four then the number must be a multiple of four.

choices.label: D
choices.content: If a number is an even number then it must be a multiple of $4$.

choices.label: E
choices.content: If the sum of all the digits in a number is a multiple of five then the number must be a multiple of five.
